channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Cambodia's leader presides at ceremony for upgrade of naval base with help from China
item.description: SIHANOUKVILLE, Cambodia (AP) — Cambodian Prime Minister Hun Manet on Saturday presided over the opening of an expansion of his country’s main naval base, which analysts and the U.S. government suspect will be used as a strategic outpost by China.
item.dc:creator: SOPHENG CHEANG, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Death toll in Russian missile strike in central Ukraine reaches 18
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — The death toll from a Russian missile strike in the central Ukrainian city of Kryvyi Rih has risen to 18, including nine children, regional governor Serhii Lysak said Saturday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Argentina's Milei doubles down on Trump bromance as the world reels from trade shock
item.description: BUENOS AIRES, Argentina (AP) — Europe vowed retaliation. China plotted tariffs of its own. Mexico scrambled to blunt the blow. But while the world's leaders were wringing their hands over President Donald Trump's announcement of sweeping tariffs on U.S.
item.dc:creator: ISABEL DEBRE, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Karen Read's second trial heads into weekend without full jury seated
item.description: Ten of 16 jurors have been selected for Karen Read's second murder trial in the death of Boston cop John O'Keefe as the court breaks for its first weekend.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Senate GOP approves framework for Trump's tax breaks and spending cuts after late-night session
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Senate Republicans plugged away overnight and into early Saturday morning to approve their multitrillion-dollar tax breaks and spending cuts framework, hurtling past hardened Democratic opposition toward what President Donald Trump calls the “big, beautiful bill” that's central to
item.dc:creator: LISA MASCARO, LEAH ASKARINAM and KEVIN FREKING, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Yoon Suk Yeol removed as South Korea's president over short-lived martial law
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — South Korea’s Constitutional Court unanimously removed Yoon Suk Yeol from office Friday, ending his tumultuous presidency and setting up a new election, four months after he threw the nation into turmoil with an ill-fated declaration of martial law.
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM and KIM TONG-HYUNG, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: More torrential rain and flash flooding expected in heavily waterlogged South and Midwest
item.description: HOPKINSVILLE, Ky. (AP) — Another round of torrential rain and flash flooding was expected to hit Saturday in parts of the South and Midwest already heavily waterlogged by days of severe storms that in some cases spawned deadly tornadoes. Round after round of heavy rains have pounded the central U.S.
item.dc:creator: GEORGE WALKER IV and BRUCE SCHREINER, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Georgia bill would compensate the wrongfully convicted and let Trump recover costs of election case
item.description: ATLANTA (AP) — A revived attempt to fix Georgia's inefficient system for compensating people wrongfully convicted of crimes almost died.
item.dc:creator: CHARLOTTE KRAMON, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Prosecutors seek 7 years in prison for disgraced ex-US Rep. George Santos in federal fraud case
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Prosecutors are seeking more than seven years in prison for disgraced former U.S. Rep. George Santos after he pleaded guilty to federal fraud and identity theft charges. The U.S.
item.dc:creator: PHILIP MARCELO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Justice Department seeks 7 year sentence for George Santos
item.description: Prosecutors said the length of the requested sentence — 87 months — reflects the serious nature of Santos’s “unparalleled” crimes.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, KREX 5

item.title: YouTube daredevil arrested by Indian authorities after visiting remote island where US missionary was killed
item.description: A YouTube influencer visiting a remote Indian island, where he allegedly tried to make illegal contact with indigenous people, has been arrested, according to global reports.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Senate vote-a-rama on Trump budget bill kicks off
item.description: The Senate on Friday night kicked off its vote-a-rama, a process that is likely to conclude with Republicans adopting the budget resolution that will serve as a blueprint for enacting President Trump's domestic agenda.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, NewsNation

item.title: Las Vegas Sphere launching 'experience' around classic film
item.description: Sphere, the famed immersive Las Vegas entertainment arena, is set to debut two new "Sphere Experiences" later this year — and one is based on a beloved classic motion picture.
item.dc:creator: Justin Walker, KREX 5

item.title: Actor Glen Powell launches organic condiments line
item.description: Actor and producer Glen Powell has launched Smash Kitchen, a line of organic condiments that includes non-GMO pantry essentials with remixed flavors, now exclusively available nationwide at Walmart stores and Walmart.com.
item.dc:creator: Erica Pauda, KRON4

item.title: Medicare won't cover anti-obesity GLP-1 drugs as Biden admin proposed
item.description: In November last year, the Biden administration proposed a new rule that would allow drugs like Wegovy and Zepbound to be covered by Medicare and Medicaid, expanding access for roughly 3.4 million Medicare users and about 4 million Medicaid enrollees.
item.dc:creator: Joseph Choi, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: JP Morgan predicts 2025 recession as Trump tariffs rattle market
item.description: President Trump's tariffs and their fallout will cause the U.S. economy to enter a recession this year, J.P. Morgan predicts.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Shopping spree? Consumers rush to buy goods ahead of Trump tariffs
item.description: President Trump's tariffs are expected to drive up prices, and some consumers are trying to get ahead of the action.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Dorn, KRON4

item.title: Texas track meet stabbing suspect told responding officer he 'did it': docs
item.description: The Texas teenager accused of fatally stabbing 17-year-old Austin Metcalf at a high school track meet on Wednesday allegedly confessed to the crime to a responding officer.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Frito-Lay recalls some Tostitos chips over undeclared allergen
item.description: Frito-Lay is recalling a small number of bags of its Tostitos Cantina Traditional Yellow Corn Tortilla Chips.
item.dc:creator: Russell Falcon, Fox 5

item.title: Dad's last-minute Christmas gift becomes $7M lotto winner
item.description: A California Lottery scratcher ticket that was given to a man as a gift by his father turned out to be a multimillion-dollar winner.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump to give TikTok another 75-day extension
item.description: President Trump said Friday that he plans to give TikTok another 75-day extension as his administration nears a deal to avert a ban on the popular social media platform.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, Fox 5

item.title: Texas measles outbreak approaches 500 cases
item.description: The deadly measles outbreak in Texas is continuing to rise, now approaching a total of 500 confirmed cases with at least 56 people facing hospitalization since late January, according to health officials.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, Fox 5

item.title: 100-year-old tortoise gives birth to critically endangered hatchlings at Philly Zoo
item.description: For the first time in over 150 years, the Philadelphia Zoo has new additions to the Western Santa Cruz Galapagos tortoise family.
item.dc:creator: Jessica Yakubovsky, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Bondi announces charges in MS-13 killing as the Trump administration highlights its gang crackdown
item.description: FORT LAUDERDALE, Fla. (AP) — Three alleged MS-13 gang members have been federally charged in connection with a killing a decade ago in Florida, U.S. Attorney General Pam Bondi announced Friday, seeking to highlight the Trump administration's push to prosecute violent gangs.
item.dc:creator: STEPHANY MATAT, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: CEO allegedly choked fellow cruise ship passenger over dance floor dispute: FBI
item.description: First American Financial CEO Kenneth DeGiorgio is facing federal charges after allegedly choking another passenger while on a Virgin Voyages cruise, according to the FBI.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Texas Pete hot sauces recalled in 10 states
item.description: The T.W. Garner Food Company issued a recall for certain bottles of its Texas Pete Habanero Buffalo Sauce and Texas Pete Sweet CHAbanero Sauce on Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Michael Reiner, WGN 9

item.title: Texas Pete hot sauces recalled in 10 states
item.description: Two varieties of Texas Pete hot sauces have been recalled in 10 states over issues with bottling and labeling.
item.dc:creator: Michael Reiner, NewsNation

item.title: Comedian Russell Brand charged with rape and sexual assault in UK
item.description: British police on Friday charged Russell Brand with rape and sexual assault following an 18-month investigation sparked when four women alleged they had been assaulted by the controversial comedian.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump ordered to pay legal fees in ‘Steele Dossier’ lawsuit
item.description: A judge in the United Kingdom has ordered President Donald Trump to pay more than $820,000 in legal fees to the company representing former British spy Christopher Steele after he unsuccessfully sued over a dossier.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: HHS staff working on childhood lead exposure and cancer clusters fired
item.description: Staff members who fought childhood lead exposure and those who worked on cancer clusters were among those who were fired from the Department of Health and Human Services (HHS), a now former employee told The Hill.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Frazin, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Boy, 13, found dead in Southern California was last with soccer coach, family says
item.description: The victim's family claims the teen was with his soccer coach in the Antelope Valley at the time of his disappearance and are now asking how his body ended up in a ditch almost 70 miles from his home.
item.dc:creator: Josh DuBose, NewsNation

item.title: Trump sued over China tariffs
item.description: It marks the first known legal challenge against Trump’s tariffs, which have fulfilled a campaign promise and rattled financial markets.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, Fox 5

item.title: What you do before and during a tornado could save your life
item.description: When a tornado takes aim at your house, and the sirens are sounding, the dogs are barking and the children are screaming, there are some last-minute precautions that could save the lives of you and your loved ones.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Women's Final Four teams touch the court for shootarounds ahead of tournament
item.description: During press conferences, the teams expressed excitement and an eagerness to fight for their spot in the championship.
item.dc:creator: Marilyn Parker, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Storms kill 7 in the South and Midwest as forecasters warn of catastrophic rains, floods this week
item.description: LAKE CITY, Ark. (AP) — Standing alongside the twisted steel tractors on his family farm in northeast Arkansas on Thursday, Danny Qualls looked on while friends and relatives helped him begin cleaning up.
item.dc:creator: ADRIAN SAINZ, GEORGE WALKER IV and JOHN SEEWER, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Demand for viral 'torpedo' bats has Pennsylvania factory in overdrive
item.description: KING OF PRUSSIA, Pa. (AP) — A 70-year-old man who plays in an area senior hardball league popped into Victus Sports this week because he needed bats for the new season.
item.dc:creator: DAN GELSTON, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Gravedigger arrested after baby's corpse found on West Virginia cemetery's grounds
item.description: A gravedigger at a Cabell County cemetery was arrested on Wednesday after a baby's corpse from 1982 and a busted casket were found at the Baylous Cemetery last week.
item.dc:creator: Isaac Taylor, ABC 4
